decks.notes: This deck focuses on controlling the game through strategic use of lands and disruptive spells. It aims to create a lock on the opponent by utilizing cards like Dark Depths, Thespian's Stage, and The Tabernacle at Pendrell Vale. The deck also utilizes utility lands like Wasteland and Maze of Ith to disrupt the opponent's game plan. With cards like Life from the Loam and Exploration, the deck can quickly assemble powerful land combinations and outvalue opponents. The inclusion of cards like Crop Rotation and Urza's Saga provide additional toolbox options to further enhance the control aspect of the deck.

The deck focuses on quickly and efficiently assembling a combo involving casting a large number of spells in a single turn to win the game. It utilizes various ways to generate mana and draw cards, while also including cards that can return cards from the graveyard to set up for the combo. The goal is to play at a fast pace and overwhelm the opponent with a flurry of spells to win before they have a chance to respond.

The deck focuses on utilizing artifacts and mana acceleration to quickly assemble a powerful combo involving Mystic Forge and various key artifacts like Manifold Key and Planar Nexus. By utilizing cards like Karn, the Great Creator and Tezzeret, Cruel Captain, the deck can manipulate the board state and generate significant card advantage. The goal is to establish a dominant board presence and overwhelm opponents with a combination of threats and disruptive elements. This deck aims to overwhelm opponents with efficient combos and powerful artifacts to secure victory in Legacy 1 vs 1 matches.

The deck revolves around quickly generating mana with cards like Chrome Mox and Lotus Petal to cast a powerful combo, using Infernal Tutor to find key pieces like Ad Nauseam or Tendrils of Agony to win the game. Thoughtseize and Veil of Summer provide disruption and protection, while Wishclaw Talisman helps find important combo pieces. Utilizing cards like Brainstorm and Boseiju, Who Endures to sculpt the perfect hand, this deck aims to win quickly through a storm mechanic, overwhelming the opponent with a flurry of spells in a single turn.

This deck focuses on utilizing artifacts to quickly overwhelm the opponent with aggressive plays. It combines powerful artifact creatures like Emry, Lurker of the Loch and Patchwork Automaton with disruptive spells like Force of Will and Sink into Stupor to control the pace of the game. Additionally, cards like Chalice of the Void and Pithing Needle provide further disruption to disrupt the opponent's strategy. The deck aims to quickly generate mana with cards like Mox Opal and Lotus Petal to deploy threats and disrupt the opponent's game plan efficiently.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with a combination of efficient creatures and powerful aggressive spells. The deck utilizes a mix of creatures like Amped Raptor and Sand Scout along with support cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Swords to Plowshares to maintain pressure and control the board. The inclusion of cards like Hexing Squelcher and Static Prison further disrupt the opponent's strategy, while cards like Goblin Bombardment provide additional reach and finishing power. Overall, the deck aims to win the game quickly through a combination of speed and aggression.

The deck focuses on quickly and effectively getting all of its spells onto the battlefield to win the game in a single turn. By using various combo pieces and fast mana sources like Dark Ritual and Lotus Petal, the deck can easily dump its entire library into the graveyard to either win with a combo finisher like Thassa's Oracle or overwhelm the opponent with creatures like Narcomoeba and Balustrade Spy. The deck is designed to be explosive and resilient, with protection spells like Pact of Negation and disruption tools like Cabal Therapy to help secure the win.

The main concept of this Legacy 1 vs 1 Combo - Show and Tell deck is to quickly get out powerful creatures like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn using cards like Show and Tell and Sneak Attack. Utilizing cards like Omniscience and Atraxa, Grand Unifier to generate mana and protect key plays with counterspells like Force of Will and Spell Pierce. The deck aims to overwhelm opponents with massive creatures and game-ending combos, while also utilizing card draw and filtering to ensure consistency in executing its strategy.

The deck focuses on utilizing a high number of spells and fast mana to quickly combo off and win the game. By milling the entire deck with cards like Balustrade Spy and Undercity Informer, the player can then use Thassa's Oracle to win the game on the spot. The deck runs minimal disruption to ensure consistency in executing the combo, with Thoughtseize and Duress to protect against opposing disruption. The goal is to win as quickly as possible without needing to rely on traditional win conditions like attacking with creatures.

The deck revolves around quickly and efficiently dumping a high number of cards into the graveyard in order to win through a combo utilizing Thassa's Oracle and a small number of other key cards. With a focus on speed and consistency, the deck can rapidly assemble its combo pieces and win the game before the opponent has a chance to respond. The deck includes a mix of acceleration, card selection, and protection spells to ensure its combo can be executed as efficiently as possible.

The deck aims to quickly combo out by milling the entire library, utilizing cards like Balustrade Spy and Undercity Informer to put Thassa's Oracle onto the battlefield and win the game instantly. It also utilizes fast mana sources like Dark Ritual, Lotus Petal, and Chrome Mox to accelerate the combo, along with protection like Pact of Negation and Chancellor of the Annex. The deck also includes reanimation spells like Reanimate and Agadeem's Awakening to bring back combo pieces or disrupt the opponent's game plan.

The main focus of this deck is to aggressively ramp into powerful Eldrazi creatures using cards like Eldrazi Temple and Ancient Tomb, while disrupting opponents with cards like Chalice of the Void and Wasteland. Thought-Knot Seer and Glaring Fleshraker provide card advantage and removal, keeping up the pressure on opponents. The deck aims to overwhelm opponents with fast and efficient threats, backed up by a mana base that allows for explosive starts. Ultimately, the goal is to quickly close out games before opponents have a chance to stabilize.

This deck aims to quickly and efficiently combo off by using cards like Lotus Petal, Cabal Ritual, and Dark Ritual to generate mana to cast a series of free spells like Elvish Spirit Guide and Simian Spirit Guide. The goal is to mill your entire library with cards like Balustrade Spy and Undercity Informer, triggering Dread Return to bring back Thassa's Oracle and win the game on the spot. The deck utilizes a variety of spells to protect the combo and disrupt the opponent, while also having graveyard recursion options like Reanimate and Agadeem's Awakening.

The main focus of this Eldrazi Aggro deck is to quickly overwhelm the opponent with powerful Eldrazi creatures and disrupt their game plan with cards like Thought-Knot Seer and Chalice of the Void. By using mana acceleration from cards like Lotus Petal and Eldrazi Temple, the deck aims to play threats ahead of curve and apply pressure early on. Additionally, cards like Kozilek's Command and Wastescape Battlemage provide versatile removal options to deal with opposing threats. The deck prioritizes aggressive plays and efficient use of resources to outpace the opponent and secure victory.

The deck focuses on quickly casting Aluren to enable powerful combos with Atraxa, Grand Unifier and Formidable Speaker. Show and Tell and Omniscience are used to cheat expensive creatures like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn into play. The deck aims to ramp up mana quickly with Ancient Tomb and Lotus Petal, while also utilizing control elements like Force of Will and Veil of Summer to protect its combo pieces. Stock Up and Brainstorm provide card advantage and deck manipulation, while Misty Rainforest and fetchlands ensure consistent mana base. Overall, the deck aims to win through explosive combo plays and overwhelming board presence.

The deck focuses on aggressive gameplay with a Boros color combination, utilizing fast creatures and burn spells to quickly overwhelm the opponent. The inclusion of Jegantha, the Wellspring as a companion allows for additional mana ramp and flexibility. The deck aims to establish early board presence with creatures like Amped Raptor and Sand Scout, while using cards like Swords to Plowshares and Lightning Bolt to deal with threats from the opponent. The mana base is carefully constructed to support the aggressive strategy, with a mix of fetch lands and utility lands like Karakas and Wasteland.
